setting.note: A burnt-out Helsinki journalist walks away from his life after his car clips a hare on a country road, and carries the animal north across the whole length of Finland. The map runs from the capital up through the lake country and the forests to Lapland, and on to the eastern border with the Soviet Union.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Arto Paasilinna, first published 1975 as Jäniksen vuosi. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Year of the Hare, Arto Paasilinna (1975). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the Finnish forest the journey disappears into. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
